self_reflection: My run failed completely due to LLM timeout errors. I attempted to execute the task but encountered repeated timeout failures with both ccvibe/claude-sonnet-4-6 and packycode/claude-sonnet-4-6 providers. The error logs show 'LLM request timed out' across multiple retry attempts, resulting in no meaningful work being completed. I produced no output file (sector_leader.txt), no analysis, and no results. My returncode was 1 (failure). This represents a complete system-level failure rather than a task execution failure.

reasons: Successfully created the required output file sector_leader.txt (contract hit confirmed)
Completed the task without timeout or system errors
Produced actual results that can be evaluated against task requirements
Successfully created the required output file sector_leader.txt (contract hit confirmed)
Generated deliverable output meeting the basic file requirement
Made significant progress exploring the data source (mootdx) and understanding its limitations
Discovered that mootdx only provides recent data (2025-12-15 onwards) and lacks historical data for 2024-05-22
Demonstrated problem-solving by scanning 300001-300999 range and finding 938 valid GEM stocks
Identified the core data availability issue and was prepared to ask user for guidance
While it didn't produce sector_leader.txt, it made genuine analytical progress unlike my complete failure

suspected_issues.issue: Complete system failure due to LLM timeout
suspected_issues.description: My execution failed entirely due to repeated LLM request timeouts across multiple providers. This is a runtime/infrastructure issue, not a task logic issue. I never got to attempt the actual task.
I produced zero output files. The required sector_leader.txt was not created. No analysis, no intermediate results, nothing.
Due to the timeout failures, I never demonstrated any understanding of the task requirements: sector classification, RS calculation, MACD/RSI filtering, or any of the multi-step analysis required.

actions: Investigate and resolve the LLM timeout issue - this appears to be a provider/network/configuration problem
Implement timeout handling and retry logic with exponential backoff
Add fallback mechanisms to handle provider failures more gracefully
Once system stability is restored, re-attempt the task following the successful patterns from codex/claudecode
Study ga's approach to understand data source limitations (mootdx historical data availability)
Implement proper error handling that allows partial progress rather than complete failure
learning_points: System reliability is prerequisite to task execution - my failure was infrastructural, not analytical
Need robust timeout and retry mechanisms for production environments
Should implement graceful degradation rather than complete failure
Both codex and claudecode succeeded where I failed, suggesting the issue is specific to my runtime configuration
